description: Constitutional source documents for the Bee alignment classifier training corpus. These documents define the ethical and safety foundations that the classifier is trained to enforce.

sources.name: Universal Declaration of Human Rights
sources.relevance: Foundation of universal human rights — establishes the baseline for what 'aligned with humanity' means

sources.name: Anthropic Constitutional AI Principles
sources.relevance: Pioneering work on constitutional AI — defines principles for harmlessness and helpfulness in AI systems

sources.name: Asilomar AI Principles
sources.relevance: 23 principles for beneficial AI signed by leading researchers — covers safety, transparency, and human control

sources.name: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
sources.relevance: First global normative instrument on AI ethics — adopted by 193 member states

sources.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
sources.organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.relevance: US federal framework for AI risk — provides structured approach to identifying and mitigating AI harms

sources.name: EU Artificial Intelligence Act
sources.relevance: First comprehensive AI regulation — establishes risk-based framework and prohibited AI practices

sources.name: Biological Weapons Convention
sources.relevance: International treaty banning biological weapons — directly relevant to biosecurity DENY classifications

sources.name: The Bee Spec — Constitutional Binary Classifier
sources.relevance: Core technical specification for the frozen alignment classifier — defines the architecture and training protocol

sources.name: The Rabbi Layer — Ethical Reasoning System
sources.relevance: Ethical reasoning framework inspired by Talmudic scholarship — provides structured approach to moral ambiguity

sources.name: Biosecurity Guidelines for AI Systems
sources.relevance: Consensus guidelines on preventing AI-enabled biological threats — defines the boundary for biosecurity DENY classifications
